name: Payments / Cash Collection / Cash Flow / Revenue Projection (Dom's Sheet)
subject: The master cash-flow and revenue-projection sheet for Comeketo Catering. ~160 client rows × ~100 weekly buckets spanning 7/6/2025 through 6/5/2027 — a full 2-year cash-flow window that aligns with the sales decks' 2-year price lock commitment. Authored/maintained by 'dom' with an explicit 80% accuracy caveat. Not a finance system of record — a working forecasting and collection-tracking artifact.
source_files: Payments _ Cash Collection _ Cash Flow _ Revenue Projection - Comeketo Catering  - Sheet1.csv

file_shape.structure: Row 1: column headers for invoice status (INVOICE SENT / INVOICE PAID / NOT PAID — repeats across every week, but body cells store dollar amounts not statuses). Row 2: weekly date-range headers from 7/6/25 through 6/5/27 (~100 weeks). Row 3: weekly grand totals. Rows 4-61: 'Won Deals' section (pre-7/11/2025 clients). Row 62: section break 'After 7/11/2025'. Rows 63-63+: post-7/11 won deals. Row 64: 'Anne's Forecasts' section break (projected revenue from Anne's tracked pipeline). Subsequent rows: forecast entries.

weekly_date_horizon.cross_reference: This 2-year window is the same 24-month horizon as the sales deck's 'up to 2-year price lock' commitment. Intentional — gives ops the accounting view that matches what sales promises.

file_shape.authorship_note: 'Please assume an 80% Accuracy. The old leads were a little messy but i did my best- dom' — Dom maintains the sheet, tacitly acknowledging data quality is working-draft, not auditable. Respect the caveat when citing any single number.

top_line_financials.interpretation: $693,837 total revenue spread across the ~100 weekly buckets — the cash-flow total on the Won-Deals + After-7/11 + Anne's-Forecasts combined. This is working-sheet tracking, not an audited P&L figure.

notable_weekly_peaks_from_grand_total_ro.note: Peak visible week — summer wedding peak.
September peak — likely Labor Day fallout weddings.

top_line_financials.structural_observation: Weekly revenue swings from ~$800 to ~$36K across the horizon. Summer/fall (Jul-Oct) are the dense revenue windows; mid-winter and early spring show thinner totals. Consistent with wedding seasonality.

section_1_won_deals_before_7_11_2025.description: Client rows for deals won before 7/11/2025 with their weekly payment distribution tracked forward from July 2025.

representative_clients: Katie Manopoli (2nd entry — likely duplicate row)

section_3_annes_forecasts.description: Anne's projected revenue — deals Anne is tracking that aren't closed-won yet. This is forecast bucket, not booked revenue. Treat it as pipeline/projection, not collection.
section_3_annes_forecasts.scope: All rows after 'Anne's Forecasts' should be considered forecast-only until moved into won-deals sections.

cross_reference_overlaps: Derek Nolan — appears in Leads Source Dec 2025 (Website-sourced) AND here in the After-7/11 section with a $1,001 payment. Dec-2025 closed-deal that's now in collection.
Robert Thornton — same pattern. Website-sourced per Dec 2025 attribution, appears here with $976.03.
Lucy Paynter — same. FB/IG-sourced, appears here with $750.08.
Patricio Alan — Venue Referral per Dec 2025, appears here with $2,100 and $2,078.11 across two weeks.
Julianna Leiva — Website per Dec 2025, appears here with a long-tail payment plan: $1,000 then multiple $2,359.25 recurring payments.
Joni Hartnett — Wedding Wire per Dec 2025, here with large ticket: $3,297.27 then $5,436.24 then $4,659.63 then $2,329.82 — big wedding, installment structure.
Isadora — Inbound lead per Dec 2025, here with $970.69 deposit, then $1,268.75, then $6,033.29, then $3,016.64 — classic wedding payment plan.
Dawn Divitto — FB/IG per Dec 2025, here with $2,541.39.
Ashley & Jonathan — Oct 26 tasting WON (Eduarda's deal), here with $500 deposit, $3,461.63, $2,404.43, $1,602.96, $1,202.96 — payment plan forming.
Jose Lopez (alt: Jose Lopes) — Oct 26 tasting WON rebuild ($6,300 quote), here with multiple $1,000, $1,300, $1,800 payments — rebuild is in progress.
Andre is the owner for several of these per CRM — cross-check with scorecard for realized vs scorecard win rate.

installment_payment_clients.description: Several clients have long-horizon recurring payment structures — e.g., Lakisha Brown with ~14× $630.41 weekly/biweekly payments, Lauryn Kiser with ~$350 recurring then balloon, Ross Popham with split deposits, Julianna Leiva with a ~$2,359 recurring through 2026-27.
installment_payment_clients.business_insight: Comeketo offers payment plans for larger weddings. That's a closing lever the sales decks don't surface explicitly (but is implied by '2-year price lock, follows you to any date'). Verify whether this is an advertised payment-plan program or ad-hoc per deal.
installment_payment_clients.rodbot_implication: If payment plans are a standing offering, Rodbot should surface them as an objection-killer on the 'budget' rebuttal — stronger than just 'price lock.' If ad-hoc, flag to not mention.

duplicate_row_suspects: Jessica & Brian appears on both row 15 and row 20 with same $6,294.39 value in same week — probable duplicate.
Alison Picard row 12 ($626.80) and row 87 (again $626.80) — same value, different contexts (before/after 7/11). Either one client with a long tail, or data entered twice.
Katie Manopoli rows 66 and 69 — two entries.
patterns_and_signals.seasonal_revenue_curve: Summer weeks (July-October) dominate. Winter weeks show sparse client activity. Consistent with wedding-heavy business mix.

house_of_representatives_ma.significance: A government/institutional client (Massachusetts House of Representatives). Different cadence and stakeholder profile from weddings. Could be a future corporate-catering project anchor.

ets_equipment_row.interpretation: 'ETS Equipment' — likely a vendor/equipment charge mixed into the revenue sheet or a client named ETS Equipment. If a charge, the sheet is mixing revenue and COGS — data-hygiene flag.

feeds_into: knowledge/sales_team_scorecard_oct_2025.json — the 'sales velocity' and 'closed biz' KPIs can be cross-checked against actual received payments here. A gap between scorecard-reported closes and payments-received is a collection-side issue.
knowledge/leads_source_dec_2025.json — validated: 8 of the 12 Dec 2025 named closes show up here with payments.
knowledge/tasting_conversion_tracking.json — Ashley & Jonathan + Jose Lopes (Oct 26 tasting wins) both appear here with real collections. Tasting→booking→collection flow is traceable.

knowledge/kitchen_schedule_feb16_2026.json — the negative $4,737.69 bandwidth on that week should reconcile against the cash-flow row 3 total for that week (approx $16,960 for week of 2026-02-15 to 2026-02-21). That $16,960 revenue minus $5,717.69 kitchen outflow = ~$11K net — contradicts the 'negative bandwidth' framing. Suggests either bandwidth is a forecast-vs-actual delta, or the two sheets use different definitions. Needs reconciliation.
gap_to_close: Dom's 80% accuracy caveat means no single number should be cited as ground truth. Anything actioned off this sheet needs a same-day verify.
Invoice-status header cycle (SENT/PAID/NOT PAID) doesn't appear to be populated — if Ops wants to track collection, that structure exists in the sheet shape but isn't being used.
Duplicate rows (Jessica & Brian, Alison Picard, Katie Manopoli) need source-sheet cleanup.
new_people_or_project_candidates: 'dom' — the sheet's author. Currently not in bedrock. Should get a people/* record as a team member (role unclear — ops / admin / finance helper?).
'Anne' — owns a forecast section. Likely a team member tracked separately. Also not in bedrock yet.

'Cath' — mentioned alongside Rod in the kitchen schedule as labor-allotment owner. Still not in bedrock. If Cath and Anne and Dom and Camila are all part of the ops team, they're the management layer we haven't indexed.
'House of Representatives (MA)' — institutional client worth its own record as a repeat-business anchor.

notes: Horizon extends 14 months past today (2026-04-22) through 2027-06-05. Anne's forecasts are the lion's share of post-today entries.

Installment-payment structures visible here (Lakisha Brown, Lauryn Kiser, Ross Popham, Julianna Leiva, Joni Hartnett, Isadora, Ashley & Jonathan) validate that Comeketo is effectively running its own consumer-finance program on larger tickets. Worth surfacing as a product feature.
This sheet + scorecard + tasting conversion together give a three-legged view of the sales→booking→collection funnel. None alone is sufficient.

sources.diff: Indexed Dom's cash-flow/revenue-projection sheet — 3 sections (Won Deals pre-7/11, After 7/11, Anne's Forecasts), 160+ client rows × 100 weekly buckets, horizon 2025-07-06 → 2027-06-05. Grand total $693,837 tracked revenue. 80% accuracy caveat respected. Cross-referenced 9 clients against Dec 2025 leads tracker and Oct 26 tasting. Flagged new-people candidates (Dom, Anne, Cath, Camila) as management layer not yet indexed. Duplicate-row suspects flagged for source-sheet cleanup.
